events.title: Iran's Asymmetric Counterair Campaign
events.desc: Iranian drones and missiles struck Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia, destroying an E-3 Sentry and damaging multiple KC-135 tankers. These strikes are part of a deliberate counterair campaign waged through asymmetric means.
events.location: Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia

events.title: Israeli Strike Kills 13 Security Personnel in Southern Lebanon
events.desc: An Israeli strike in the southern Lebanese city of Nabatiyeh killed 13 State Security personnel. This attack occurred as Lebanon prepared to start ceasefire talks with Israel, prompting condemnation and a strengthened resolve for a ceasefire.

events.title: Wave of Israeli Strikes Hits Lebanon Post-Ceasefire Announcement
events.desc: Hours after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced, Israel launched a large wave of strikes across Lebanon, hitting areas including the southern suburbs of Beirut and southern Lebanon. The attacks resulted in significant casualties.

events.title: US-Iran Peace Talks Begin in Pakistan Amidst Significant Hurdles
events.desc: US-Iran peace talks have commenced in Islamabad, Pakistan, with mediators focusing on maintaining dialogue despite deep divisions between the two nations. Challenges include a deficit of trust and differing proposals for ending the conflict.

events.title: Israeli Settlements Approved in West Bank Amidst International Condemnation
events.desc: Israel approved the construction of 34 new settlements in the occupied West Bank. The Palestinian Presidency condemned the move as a 'flagrant violation of international law,' and the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) also condemned the approval.

events.title: US Military Deploys Mines Over Iranian Village
events.desc: Evidence suggests the US deployed the Gator Scatterable Mine system over Kafari, a village near Shiraz in southern Iran. Iranian media reported casualties, and experts identified the munitions as US-made anti-tank mines.

events.title: Iran Imposes Near-Total Internet Blackout Amidst Strikes
events.desc: Following Israeli and American strikes, Iran's internet traffic collapsed by 98 percent. The government deliberately dismantled communication infrastructure, halting internet traffic to control information flow during the strikes on military installations.

events.title: Lebanon and Israel Prepare for Direct Talks Amidst Ongoing Conflict
events.desc: Lebanon's President Joseph Aoun called for direct talks with Israel following a month of intense fighting. While Lebanon insists on a ceasefire before talks, Israel's Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has agreed to meet, aiming to de-escalate the conflict.

events.title: Inflation Rises in US Amid Iran War and Hormuz Blockade
events.desc: A government report indicates a significant rise in inflation in the US, with gasoline prices increasing by 21.2 percent in March. This economic impact is linked to the ongoing Iran war and the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: US Vice President Vance Expresses Optimism for Iran Talks, Warns Against Deception
events.desc: US Vice President JD Vance stated he anticipates positive negotiations with Iranian officials in Pakistan, emphasizing that Tehran should not attempt to 'play' the United States during the talks. Vance's departure for Islamabad signals a continued diplomatic effort.

events.title: French Forward Deterrence Doctrine Announced
events.desc: French President Emmanuel Macron unveiled a new doctrine of 'forward deterrence,' including the unprecedented offer to deploy French Strategic Air Forces to European allied countries. This move aims to enhance deterrence by dispersing nuclear forces.

events.title: US Air Force Interceptors Downed Iranian Drones in Middle East
events.desc: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy stated that Ukrainian interceptors, operated by 228 Ukrainian experts deployed in the region, successfully downed Iranian drones in the Middle East. This highlights international cooperation in air defense.

events.title: Hungarian Government Passwords Exposed Online
events.desc: Approximately 800 Hungarian government email addresses and passwords have been leaked online, affecting 12 out of 13 ministries. This vulnerability could expose sensitive and classified information.
